images.prompt:
[SUBJECT] A split-frame schematic diptych that puts two pictures of knowing side by side: on the left, the receiver picture drawn as a strict one-way arrow running from a finished lit world into a disembodied eye-cone, with the return-path struck through by a diagonal engineering strike-out line marking it as an impossible diagram; on the right, the probe-loop picture drawn as a symmetric coupling between an instrument and a target, with two oppositely oriented arrows tracing the emission outward and the return inward, wrapped by a closed feedback contour.

Foreground Color: #CA8F31 industrial yellow main color in foreground

Background: Flat black #000000

Positive Prompt:

A split-frame schematic diptych. Left panel: the receiver picture as a one-way arrow from a finished lit world into a disembodied eye-cone, with the return-path struck through by a diagonal engineering strike-out line marking it as an impossible diagram. Right panel: the probe-loop picture as a symmetric coupling between an instrument and its target, with two opposed arrows tracing emission and return, enclosed by a closed feedback contour. The two panels share identical framing, line weight, and hierarchy so the structural rhyme reads as a single comparison plate. Illustrated as a hyper-detailed technical sketch with dense linework, mechanical schematic motifs, exploded-view call-outs, and blueprint precision. Add signal-flow diagram aesthetic, engineering strike-through annotation, paired panel framing. Minimalist layout inspired by Swiss International Typographic Style and IBM ThinkPad aesthetics. Strong geometric structure, asymmetric composition, and industrial futurism. Strictly use color palette: industrial yellow #CA8F31 in foreground on flat black #000000 background. High contrast, figure-ground separation, negative space balanced with zones of extreme density. Crisp edges, poster-like composition.
